title: Fix video card to fit fully within mobile viewport height

requirements.summary: Constrain VideoPostCard layout to fit within mobile viewport height without overflow
acceptanceCriteria: On a typical mobile viewport (e.g. 390x844 or 360x800), the full video card — header, title, video, and action row — is visible without scrolling within the card.
The video element uses object-fit: contain or cover and does not overflow below the bottom of the viewport.
Each card takes up exactly the available viewport height (100dvh minus header height), so users can scroll through one card at a time.
The layout works in both portrait and landscape mobile orientations.

file_operations.description: Refactor the VideoPostCard component to use a flex column layout with a fixed height constraint of calc(100dvh - header height). Set the video element to flex-grow with object-fit: contain and max-height to prevent overflow. Ensure the card header (avatar, username, date), title, video, and action buttons all fit within the calculated viewport height. Add a wrapper div with overflow-hidden to enforce the boundary. Test with typical mobile viewports (390x844, 360x800) in both portrait and landscape.

Update the HomePage feed container to use snap-y snap-mandatory scroll behavior so each VideoPostCard occupies exactly one viewport height and users can scroll through cards one at a time. Ensure each card wrapper has snap-start or snap-center applied. Adjust spacing and padding so cards align cleanly within the viewport.
